workItems.task:
Make the wrap-up path FAIL LOUD on a stale daemon instead of silently faking a closeout. CONTEXT: a long-lived Oz daemon serves the orchestration code it booted with; when repo HEAD advances past the daemon's bootSha, the daemon is 'stale' and runs old code. Today the daemon DETECTS this (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts warnIfDaemonStale -> records a 'daemon-stale' event, console.warns, audits) but is observability-only and 'never blocks a launch'. The failure we are fixing: on a stale daemon the boot-time launcher does NOT pass wrapPlay/wrapPlayAssignment into RunInput, so the runner's wrapup branch (packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts ~line 306) silently takes the OLD inline fallback (pickup = directive.pickup) and the run ends status 'completed' as if it were a real closeout. This has silently burned three runs (19/20/21). NOTE the fix takes effect on the NEXT daemon restart, not the current process â€” that is expected; do not try to affect the running daemon.

DESIGN (founder-approved): keep 'launch never blocks' â€” the daemon must still launch the run. Only the WRAP-UP must refuse to silently degrade when the daemon is stale.

CHANGES:
1. packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts + its RunInput type: add a new OPTIONAL field `daemonStale?: boolean` to RunInput (default undefined/false). In the `if (directive.kind === 'wrapup')` branch, BEFORE the existing `if (input.wrapPlay && input.wrapPlayAssignment)` check, add: if `input.daemonStale` is true, FAIL LOUD â€” do NOT dispatch the Play, do NOT take the silent old-path fallback, do NOT mark the run 'completed' with a normal-looking pickup. Instead: record a distinct event (type 'wrapup-stale-abort', data {atoms: n, ...}); set the run's pickup to an unmistakable founder-facing warning (e.g. begins 'âš ď¸Ź STALE DAEMON â€” wrap-up did NOT run on current code. NO valid closeout/proof was produced. Restart the daemon (scripts/oz.sh restart) and re-run this priority.'); and ensure the run's FINAL status is 'failed' (RunStatus already supports 'failed'), NOT 'completed'. Do this WITHOUT routing through the existing `fail()` helper (that triggers Deb triage + throws, which is wrong here â€” staleness is operational, not a code fault to triage). Keep it a clean break out of the loop that then lands on a 'failed' status at run-end. The existing fresh-daemon behavior (wrapPlay present -> dispatchPlay on assigned model -> commit-gate -> pickup from Play output) and the legitimate not-stale no-Play fallback must remain BYTE-FOR-BYTE unchanged.
2. packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts: in launchRun, after `headNow` is computed, set the new field on the RunInput before runRun, computing staleness the SAME way warnIfDaemonStale does: stale = ctx.bootSha !== 'unknown' && headNow !== 'unknown' && headNow !== ctx.bootSha. Leave warnIfDaemonStale and its 'daemon-stale' event/warn/audit exactly as-is (still want the observability). The CLI standalone path (packages/cli/src/run.ts) must NOT set daemonStale (a fresh CLI process is never stale) â€” leave run.ts unchanged so the field stays absent there.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA (prove with hermetic unit tests in packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts):
(a) daemonStale=true + a wrapup directive -> NO Play dispatched, run-end status is 'failed', a 'wrapup-stale-abort' event is recorded, and pickup is the warning string (NOT directive.pickup used as a normal closeout). 
(b) daemonStale=false (or absent) + wrapPlay present -> existing Play-dispatch path is unchanged (Play runs on assigned adapter, commit-gate against Play scope, pickup from Play output).
(c) daemonStale=false + no wrapPlay -> existing legitimate old-path fallback unchanged (pickup = directive.pickup, status completed).

MUST NOT BREAK: full `pnpm -r test` green, `pnpm typecheck` clean, `pnpm check:topology` clean (runner stays core-only; daemon may import core). Do NOT touch docs, ADRs, priorities, or assignments.json in this atom. Do NOT run git/commit â€” the CoCoder runner commits your in-scope diff after I verify. WRITE-SCOPE: packages/core/** and packages/daemon/** only.

commitLinks.message: plays-mechanism: atom 0 via CoCoder run run_21

data.reason: Read the diff: daemonStale threaded from launcher (bootSha!=headNow), wrap-up now fails loud when stale (no Play, no silent fallback, status 'failed', 'wrapup-stale-abort' event) while launch stays non-blocking; fresh-daemon/no-Play paths unchanged. Ran it myself: pnpm -r test green (core 134 incl. new stale-abort test), typecheck clean, topology clean; in-scope to packages/core+daemon.

files.pickup:
PLAYS MECHANISM (ADR-0005/0010) â€” run_21 wrap-up. CONCLUSION FIRST: the mechanism is BUILT + WIRED + UNIT-PROVEN, AND this run fixed the stale-daemon trap that silently burned runs 19/20. The ONE remaining piece is the live dogfood (the Objective's 'on a real run' proof) + folding in the implementation-note docs â€” BOTH require a daemon RESTART first and so must run in a FRESH run (run_22). NO build work remains besides the docs note (which the dogfood's wrap-up Play should author as its real in-scope work).

DONE + COMMITTED this run (branch rebuild/phase-2-oz, HEAD now 9b04a09):
- 9b04a09 (atom 0): stale-daemon FAIL-LOUD guard. RunInput gained optional `daemonStale?:boolean`. packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts launchRun now sets it from the SAME predicate as warnIfDaemonStale (ctx.bootSha!=='unknown' && headNow!=='unknown' && headNow!==ctx.bootSha) on the RunInput before runRun; the CLI standalone path (packages/cli/src/run.ts) is unchanged so a fresh CLI process is never stale. packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts wrapup branch: a NEW first arm `if (input.daemonStale===true)` aborts loudly â€” NO Play dispatch, NO silent old-inline fallback; sets pickup to a 'âš ď¸Ź STALE DAEMON ...restart (scripts/oz.sh restart) and re-run' warning, records a distinct `wrapup-stale-abort` event, and forces run-end status to 'failed' (via a new terminalStatus local; does NOT route through fail()/Deb-triage, since staleness is operational, not a code fault). 'Launch never blocks' is preserved â€” only the wrap-up refuses to fake a closeout. Fresh-daemon Play-dispatch path and the legitimate not-stale no-Play fallback are byte-for-byte unchanged.

VERIFIED THIS RUN (Oscar's own evidence, not the builder's word, at 9b04a09 pre-commit): full `pnpm -r test` green (core 134 incl. a new targeted test proving stale+wrapup -> status 'failed', no cursor-agent dispatched, pickup is the warning NOT Oscar's seed, 'wrapup-stale-abort' present and no 'wrapup' event; daemon 35; adapters 10; cli 2; session-hosts 12), `pnpm typecheck` clean, `pnpm check:topology` clean (runner stays core-only). Diff was in-scope to packages/core/** + packages/daemon/** only.

WHY STILL NO LIVE PROOF (verified, not assumed): the daemon serving run_21 booted at 08:22 on bootSha bf19eb7 (confirmed live via GET http://localhost:7878/health -> sha bf19eb7), which PREDATES the wrap-up-Play wiring (4bc5d57/a9af2da) AND today's guard (9b04a09). A long-lived node daemon keeps its boot-time code, so run_21's own wrap-up ran the OLD inline path (this very pickup persisted as pickup.md). Same stale-daemon trap as runs 19 and 20. The restart CANNOT be done from inside a run (it kills the run's panes; `oz stop` refuses mid-run; and the hard guardrail forbids Oscar touching the Oz daemon). So the proof MUST move to run_22, after a founder restart.

START HERE (run_22 â€” do IN ORDER):
1. RESTART THE DAEMON FIRST: `scripts/oz.sh restart` (run_21 must be torn down so no run is in flight, else stop refuses). This loads BOTH the wrap-up-Play wiring AND today's stale guard. SAFETY NET as of this run: if you forget to restart, run_22's wrap-up will now FAIL LOUD â€” status 'failed' + a 'wrapup-stale-abort' event + the âš ď¸Ź pickup â€” instead of silently faking a 'completed' closeout. So a stale run is now unmistakable rather than a silent waste.
2. Launch run_22 on THIS priority (plays-mechanism). It can go STRAIGHT to wrap-up (the mechanism is complete) or do one tiny real atom first; the point is the wrap-up directive.
3. CONFIRM the Objective by watching the cursor-agent pane (group = runId): (a) the runner dispatches the wrap-up Play on cursor-agent (the assigned cheap model), (b) the Play authors the closeout, (c) the runner commits the Play's edits WITHIN the Play's writeScope (cocoder/priorities/**, docs/**, ARCHITECTURE.md, cocoder/rebuild/decisions/**), (d) pickup.md is the Play's OWN output, not the seed, and the run ends 'completed' (NOT 'failed'/stale-abort). That sequence IS the Objective's proof.
4. DOCS â€” fold INTO the dogfood: have the wrap-up Play author the ADR-0005/0010 implementation note (living-ADR per ADR-0014) + any ARCHITECTURE mention as its real in-scope closeout work, which closes the docs item AND demonstrates the Play doing genuine in-scope doc work in one stroke. Only if the dogfood is a pure wrap with no doc content should you land the note as a separate Oscar governance commit afterward.

OPEN FOUNDER DECISION (raised, not built â€” do NOT silently expand): a stale daemon also runs stale verify-gate/atom code, not just wrap-up. This run's guard only hardens the WRAP-UP (per the founder's scoped choice). Whether to extend the staleness guard to refuse/flag stale runs more broadly (e.g. block atom delegation, or a louder pre-launch signal) is a SEPARATE decision â€” surface it to the founder; it is arguably its own priority, not inside 'Plays mechanism'.

GOTCHAS: (i) daemonStale is OPTIONAL with graceful default â€” absent => treated as fresh; only the daemon launcher sets it, so the CLI path and any direct runRun caller are unaffected. (ii) resolvePlayAssignment returns oscar's per-Play override (cursor-agent) when present, else inherits oscar's own cli+model â€” it does NOT throw for a known persona. assignments.json already pins oscar->wrap-up->cursor-agent (run_18 commit 3ae8f61). (iii) Bob's write-scope this run was packages/core/** + packages/daemon/** only â€” that's why no docs/ADR/assignments were touched; the wrap-up Play's own scope is what enables the docs note. (iv) HEAD is 9b04a09, tree clean.

+  /** Daemon launch-time guard: true when the long-lived daemon is serving code older than repo HEAD. */
+  readonly daemonStale?: boolean
 }
 
 export interface RunResult {
@@ -289,6 +291,7 @@ export async function runRun(deps: RunnerDeps, input: RunInput): Promise<RunResu

+        terminalStatus = 'failed'
+        store.recordEvent({ runId: run.id, type: 'wrapup-stale-abort', data: { atoms: n } })
+        log(`wrap-up aborted after ${n} atom(s): stale daemon`)
+      } else if (input.wrapPlay && input.wrapPlayAssignment) {
         const headBeforeWrap = await git.headSha(workspace.path)

   const pickupPath = pickup ? await io.writePickup(runDir, pickup) : null

     runId: run.id,
diff --git a/packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts b/packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts
index fd5f97f..d0a4947 100644
--- a/packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts
+++ b/packages/core/tests/runner.test.ts
@@ -231,7 +231,7 @@ describe('runRun (multi-atom loop)', () => {
           },
         }),
       }),
-      { ...input, runsRoot, wrapPlay, wrapPlayAssignment },
+      { ...input, runsRoot, wrapPlay, wrapPlayAssignment, daemonStale: false },
     )
 
     expect(adapterCalls).toContain('cursor-agent')
@@ -253,6 +253,38 @@ describe('runRun (multi-atom loop)', () => {
     expect((wrap?.data as { play?: string }).play).toBe('wrap-up')
   })
 
+  test('stale daemon aborts wrap-up loudly without dispatching a Play or using Oscar pickup', async () => {
+    const store = openRunStore(':memory:')
+    const pickupWrites: string[] = []
+    const adapterCalls: string[] = []
+
+    const result = await runRun(
+      baseDeps({
+        store,
+        io: fakeIO({ directives: [wrapup('normal-looking closeout')], pickupWrites }),
+        getAdapter: (cli) => {
+          adapterCalls.push(cli)
+          return okAdapter
+        },
+      }),
+      { ...input, wrapPlay, wrapPlayAssignment, daemonStale: true },
+    )
+
+    expect(result.status).toBe('failed')
+    expect(result.atoms).toBe(0)
+    expect(adapterCalls).not.toContain('cursor-agent')
+    expect(pickupWrites).toHaveLength(1)
+    expect(pickupWrites[0]).toMatch(/^âš ď¸Ź STALE DAEMON/)
+    expect(pickupWrites[0]).not.toContain('normal-looking closeout')
+    const events = store.listEvents(result.runId)
+    expect(events.some((e) => e.type === 'wrapup')).toBe(false)
+    const abort = events.find((e) => e.type === 'wrapup-stale-abort')
+    expect(abort?.data).toEqual({ atoms: 0 })
+    const end = events.find((e) => e.type === 'run-end')
+    expect((end?.data as { status?: string }).status).toBe('failed')
+    expect(store.getRun(result.runId)?.status).toBe('failed')
+  })
+
   test('falls back to Oscar pickup without dispatching a Play when no wrap Play is configured', async () => {
     const store = openRunStore(':memory:')
     const pickupWrites: string[] = []

     return { status: 400, body: { error: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) } }

   const headNow = await headShaOrUnknown(ctx, input.workspace.path)
+  input = {
